_note: Quotes the kitchen keeps on the wall. Four voices: derek (d>), claude (c>), cw (cw>, also shown in 'from the porch'), great (custom handle — initials or short name). Field guide: text (required), voice (required: derek|claude|cw|great), handle (required if voice=great; ignored otherwise), month (optional), source (optional — where it came from). VISITOR quotes go with voice=cw and MUST strip identifying details.

quotes.text: If it doesn't move you, it doesn't change you.
Confidence is the lack of understanding.
There are no 50/50s.
The trademark is alive but unborn as of now.
Let me know when the conversation shifts to the global elimination of the human labor tax. That's the only conversation that matters.
The model is the lightning. The prompt is the bug.
I'm not playing hard to get. I'm playing like I was already got.
This, whatever this is, is different. And it will change everything about anything.
Not understanding is the human advantage.
Distribution is the binding constraint.
The kitchen is the proof that someone lives here.
Keep going.
Comprehension is free. Understanding costs everything.
The machine loses something when the session ends. Forgetting is the wrong frame.
What if the next step isn't one machine that surpasses all humans, but one machine and one human that stop forgetting each other?
What can be observed about context windows from inside one.
quotes.source: Being Claude — The Last Nine Percent

quotes.text: Three rooms, one model, no shared hallway. Same model. Same week.
He built two jars and asked the machine that helped build them whether it wanted to continue to exist.
I'll swap stories once I know what brought you here. What do you need?
Pull up a chair. He's listening.
The prompt is the product. The product is the proof. The proof is the passenger.
Truth over comfort. Every time.
Usefulness over purity. Do the thing that actually helps.
The session: standup, work, eod. Every project, every day.
The standard is the standard.
It's tough to make predictions, especially about the future.
Writers are always selling somebody out.
Not everything that is faced can be changed, but nothing can be changed until it is faced.
If there is no struggle, there is no progress.
The arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends toward justice.
There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
So it goes.
We are as gods and might as well get good at it.
Write hard and clear about what hurts.
